# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request CI

on:
  pull_request_target:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout PR code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2
      with:
        ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v4
      with:
        python-version: '3.8'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt

    - name: Run tests
      run: pytest

    - name: Post test results as comment
      uses: actions/github-script@v5
      with:
        script: |
          const { context, github } = require('@actions/github');
          const octokit = new github.GitHub(context.token);

          async function postComment() {
            try {
              await octokit.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.payload.pull_request.number,
                body: 'PR tests completed successfully.'
              });
            } catch (error) {
              console.error('Error posting comment:', error);
            }
          }

          postComment();
